updated: Apr 1, 2026 (seed data — live updates begin after GitHub setup)

items.title: Express Entry Draw #293: 847 Invitations, CRS Cut-off 524
items.summary: Latest all-program draw issued 847 ITAs. CRS dropped 6 points to 524, tracking IRCC's 485,000 PR target for 2026. Category-based draws for STEM and healthcare expected Q2.

items.title: Study Permit Cap Extended Through 2026 — New Provincial Allocations Live
items.summary: Cap maintained for 2026 intake. New allocations prioritize graduate-level programs and co-op streams. Several Ontario and BC institutions are already at capacity. Stricter document requirements in effect.

items.title: PGWP Processing Now Averaging 17 Weeks
items.summary: Post-graduation work permit processing has extended to 17 weeks average. Maintained status applies to graduates who applied before their study permit expired. IRCC recommends applying immediately after graduation.

items.title: Rural & Northern Immigration Pilot Expands to 14 New Communities
items.summary: RNIP now covers 14 additional communities across Manitoba, Saskatchewan, and Northern Ontario. Employer endorsement remains central. Occupations list updated to reflect regional labour market needs.

items.title: Arrive Launches Newcomer Mortgage Prep Tool — Immigration Gap Visible
items.summary: New feature focuses on financial readiness only. Does not address PGWP validity, work authorization status, or immigration pathway — the actual blockers for newcomer mortgage approvals. Clear content gap for BorderPass to own.

items.title: "Top 10 Jobs for New Immigrants" Driving Strong Organic Traffic
items.summary: High-engagement content. Key gap: no connection between job type and required immigration pathway or work permit eligibility. BorderPass can own the immigration-integrated version of this evergreen content.

items.title: CanadaVisa Processing Time Tracker Hits 45K Monthly Visits
items.summary: High-traffic tool with no student-specific variant and no interpretation layer. Opportunity: weekly processing time updates with meaning, not just raw numbers. Tracker + commentary = differentiated tool.

items.title: "Can I work while my PGWP is processing?" — 847 upvotes, 312 comments
items.summary: Top post this week. Most answers are incomplete or contradictory. Correct answer: yes, via maintained status, with specific conditions most people miss. High-urgency content opportunity — own this answer definitively.

items.title: "Is a Canadian degree still worth it?" — 1.2K upvotes, 489 comments
items.summary: Growing anxiety about Canada's value proposition. Key concerns: cost, permit uncertainty, post-graduation pathways. Mixed-to-negative sentiment trending. A trust-building opportunity — answer honestly with data.

items.title: "My study permit was refused for this reason" — 2.3M views
items.summary: Viral TikTok on refusal reasons. Top comment: 'How do I know if mine will be refused?' No immigration brand has answered this clearly. Refusal prevention content converts strongly for BorderPass's funnel.

items.title: "My employer wants to hire me full-time but doesn't know how" — 540 comments
items.summary: International grads struggling to help employers navigate work authorization. Strong signal for employer-facing content about PGWP-based hiring mechanics. Dual-audience opportunity (student + employer content).

items.title: International Student Enrollment Down 11% — First Decline in 12 Years
items.summary: Cap policies and economic concerns drive historic enrollment drop. Employer talent pipelines dependent on international graduates are beginning to contract. The data story for B2B audiences is compelling and available.

items.title: 67% of Canadian Employers Struggle to Retain International Graduates
items.summary: CBC survey of 320 employers: immigration uncertainty is the primary retention barrier, ahead of salary and culture. This validates BorderPass's employer value proposition with third-party data at scale.

items.title: DLI Compliance Audits Start Q2 — International Student Support in Scope
items.summary: Designated learning institutions face new auditing on international student support starting April 2026. Schools that can't demonstrate adequate support risk DLI status. Institutional partnership angle for BorderPass.
